results.question: How do I create a RingCentral Webinar using the API? What key settings are available, and how do I add registrants for a marketing webinar?
results.ground_truth: Create a webinar via the REST API specifying settings including: registrationEnabled (true for marketing webinars — gives each attendee a unique join URL), recordingEnabled, autoRecord, qnaEnabled, pollsEnabled, password, attendeeAuthentication. After creating the webinar, create a session. To add registrants for a marketing webinar, call the create registrant endpoint. Requires EditWebinars and ReadWebinars permissions. Set registrationEnabled=false for internal all-hands webinars (single shared join URL).

raw.answer:
## Overview

Creating a RingCentral Webinar via the API is a **two-step process**:

1. **Create the webinar** — a container with title, description, and settings.
2. **Create a session** — a scheduled occurrence (date/time) that hosts, panelists, and registrants attach to.

For a **marketing webinar**, set `registrationEnabled: true` so each attendee gets a unique join URL for tracking.

---

## Prerequisites

1. Create a REST API app in the [Developer Console](https://developers.ringcentral.com/) with:
   - **Permissions:** `EditWebinars`, `ReadWebinars`
   - **Auth:** JWT (typical for server-side integrations)
2. Authenticate with the RingCentral SDK or a Bearer token before calling Webinar endpoints.

---

## Step 1: Create the webinar

**Endpoint:** `POST /webinar/configuration/v1/webinars`  
**Permission:** `EditWebinars`

```http
POST /webinar/configuration/v1/webinars
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "title": "Product Launch Q3 2026",
  "description": "Quarterly product update webinar",
  "settings": {
    "recordingEnabled": true,
    "autoRecord": false,
    "registrationEnabled": true,
    "panelistWaitingRoom": true,
    "attendeeAuthentication": "Guest",
    "password": "s3cur3pass",
    "qnaEnabled": true,
    "pollsEnabled": true,
    "postWebinarRedirectUri": "https://acme.com/thank-you"
  }
}
```

Using the Node.js SDK:

```ts
const result = await rc.webinar().configuration().v1().webinars().post(webinarCreationRequest);
```

---

## Step 2: Schedule a session

**Endpoint:** `POST /webinar/configuration/v1/webinars/{webinarId}/sessions`

Required session fields:

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `scheduledStartTime` | ISO 8601 start time |
| `scheduledDuration` | Duration in seconds (30–43,200) |
| `timeZone` | IANA time zone (e.g. `America/New_York`) |
| `localeCode` | Locale (e.g. `en-US`) |

```ts
const result = await rc.webinar().configuration().v1().webinars(webinarId).sessions().post(sessionModel);
```

---

## Key webinar settings

These are the most commonly used settings (full list in the [API Reference](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/webinar)):

| Setting | Description |
|---|---|
| `recordingEnabled` | Enables recording; must be `true` for other recording settings |
| `autoRecord` | Starts recording when the webinar goes live |
| `panelistWaitingRoom` | Places panelists in a waiting room on join |
| `attendeeAuthentication` | `Guest`, `AuthenticatedUser`, or `AuthenticatedCoworker` |
| `password` | Attendee access password |
| `qnaEnabled` | Enables Q&A |
| `pollsEnabled` | Enables polls |
| **`registrationEnabled`** | **`true` for marketing webinars** (unique join URL per attendee); **`false` for internal all-hands** (single shared join URL) |
| `postWebinarRedirectUri` | Redirect URL after the webinar ends |

Additional settings in the SDK include `panelistVideoEnabled`, `panelistScreenSharingEnabled`, `qnaAnonymousEnabled`, `moderatedQnaEnabled`, `pstnEnabled`, and `externalLivestreamEnabled`.

### Internal vs. marketing webinars

- **Internal (`registrationEnabled: false`):** One shared join URL; attendees enter their name on join. No registrant API needed.
- **Marketing (`registrationEnabled: true`):** Each attendee gets a unique join URL for tracking and analytics. Use the registrant API to add attendees.

---

## Step 3: Add registrants (marketing webinars)

**Endpoint:** `POST /webinar/registration/v1/sessions/{sessionId}/registrants`  
**Operation:** [rcwRegCreateRegistrant](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/Registrants/rcwRegCreateRegistrant)  
**Permission:** `EditWebinars`

```http
POST /webinar/registration/v1/sessions/{sessionId}/registrants
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "firstName": "Jane",
  "lastName": "Smith",
  "email": "jane@example.com",
  "externalId": "lid=123&oid=456",
  "source": "customer-invite"
}
```

Required fields: `firstName`, `lastName`, `email`.

### Useful optional fields

| Field | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `externalId` | Link to a CRM contact, lead, or opportunity (can encode multiple IDs as a query string) |
| `source` | Campaign attribution (e.g. `"customer-invite"`, `"search-ad"`) |
| `joinUri` / `cancellationUri` | Custom join/cancel pages on your site instead of RingCentral-hosted pages |
| `questionnaire` | Answers to custom registration questions |

### Custom registration flow

You can host your own sign-up form, then call the create-registrant API from your backend. Set `suppressEmails: true` via the [update registration session](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/Registration-Management/rcwRegUpdateSession) endpoint (`PUT .../sessions/{sessionId}/registration`) if you want to send your own confirmation emails.

### Tracking attendance

- Each registrant gets a unique `id`.
- On join, they receive a `participantId`.
- **Attended:** both `id` and `participantId` are present.
- **No-show:** `participantId` is absent.

You can also subscribe to registrant webhooks at `POST /webinar/v1/subscriptions` with filter `/webinar/registration/v1/company/sessions/registrants`.

---

## Full workflow summary

```
1. POST /webinar/configuration/v1/webinars              → create webinar (registrationEnabled: true)
2. POST /webinar/configuration/v1/webinars/{id}/sessions → schedule session
3. POST /webinar/registration/v1/sessions/{id}/registrants → add each attendee
4. (Optional) POST .../sessions/{id}/invitees         → invite hosts/cohosts/panelists
```

For more detail, see the repo docs under `docs/webinar/` — especially [creation.mdx](docs/webinar/creation.mdx) and [registrants.mdx](docs/webinar/registrants.mdx).

mintlify.answer:
Here is how to create a RingCentral Webinar via the API, configure it for a marketing use case, and add registrants — based on the [RingCentral Webinar documentation](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/webinar/index).

## Overview: Webinar vs. Session

RingCentral separates these concepts:

- **Webinar** — a container for settings and behavior (title, recording, registration, Q&A, etc.).
- **Webinar session** — a specific scheduled occurrence (date/time) that attendees register for and join.

During the current beta, each webinar is limited to **one session**. See [Webinars and webinar sessions](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/webinar/sessions).

---

## Prerequisites

1. **RingCentral Webinar license** — required to call Webinar APIs (purchase via Admin Portal, or apply to the [Webinar Partner Developer Program](https://forms.gle/dMRNtnuFnonLQgsu7) for a free developer license).
2. **Developer app** — create a REST API app with **JWT auth flow** and permissions such as **`EditWebinars`** and **`ReadWebinars`**. See the [Webinar Quick Start](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/webinar/quick-start#create-an-app).
3. **JWT credential** — create a personal JWT in the Developer Console and store it as `RC_USER_JWT`.
4. **Environment variables** (from the [env template](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ringcentral/ringcentral-api-docs/main/code-samples/env-template)):
   - `RC_APP_CLIENT_ID`
   - `RC_APP_CLIENT_SECRET`
   - `RC_USER_JWT`
   - `RC_SERVER_URL` (typically `https://platform.ringcentral.com`)

### SDK install commands

| Language | Package | Install |
|----------|---------|---------|
| JavaScript/Node | `@ringcentral/sdk` | `npm install @ringcentral/sdk dotenv` |
| Python | `ringcentral` | `pip3 install ringcentral` |
| PHP | `ringcentral/ringcentral-php` | `php composer.phar require ringcentral/ringcentral-php` |
| Ruby | `ringcentral-sdk` | `gem install ringcentral-sdk` |

Java and C# SDKs do **not** currently support Webinar APIs; call the REST endpoints directly instead.

Authenticate with JWT, then pass the access token in the **`Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`** header on each request.

---

## Step 1: Create the Webinar

**Operation:** create webinar  
**Endpoint:** `POST https://platform.ringcentral.com/webinar/configuration/v1/webinars`

Required fields: **`title`**, **`description`**. You can also pass webinar settings in the same request.

```javascript
platform.post('/webinar/configuration/v1/webinars', {
  title: "Q2 Product Launch Webinar",
  description: "Marketing webinar for external prospects",
  registrationEnabled: true,   // required for marketing webinars
  recordingEnabled: true,
  autoRecord: true,
  qnaEnabled: true,
  pollsEnabled: true,
  postWebinarRedirectUri: "https://acme.com/thank-you"
})
```

Save the returned **`webinarId`** from the response.

---

## Step 2: Schedule a Webinar Session

**Endpoint:** `POST https://platform.ringcentral.com/webinar/configuration/v1/webinars/{webinarId}/sessions`

```javascript
platform.post('/webinar/configuration/v1/webinars/' + WEBINAR_ID + '/sessions', {
  scheduledStartTime: "2023-05-10T09:00:00.000Z",
  scheduledDuration: 7200,        // seconds (2 hours)
  timezone: "America/New_York"
})
```

Save the returned **`sessionId`**.

---

## Key Webinar Settings

These are the most commonly used settings when creating a webinar ([Webinar settings](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/webinar/creation#webinar-settings)). The full list is in the [Webinar API Reference](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/webinar).

| Setting | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `recordingEnabled` | Enables recording. If `false`, other recording settings cannot be set. |
| `autoRecord` | Starts recording automatically when the webinar goes live. |
| `panelistWaitingRoom` | Whether panelists go to a waiting room after joining. |
| `attendeeAuthentication` | Attendee auth requirement. Values: `Guest`, `AuthenticatedUser`, `AuthenticatedCoworker` |
| `password` | Password attendees must enter to join. |
| `qnaEnabled` | Turns Q&A on/off. |
| `pollsEnabled` | Turns polls on/off. |
| `registrationEnabled` | **`true` for marketing webinars**, `false` for internal/all-hands meetings. |
| `postWebinarRedirectUri` | URL to redirect attendees when the webinar ends. |

### Marketing vs. internal webinars

For **marketing webinars** (external attendees), set **`registrationEnabled: true`**. Each attendee gets a **unique join URL** for tracking and analytics.

For **internal/all-hands** meetings, set **`registrationEnabled: false`**. All attendees share one join URL and only enter their name at join time — no registrant API is needed.

---

## Step 3: Add Registrants (Marketing Webinar)

Registrant APIs are intended for **marketing webinars** with external attendees ([Registrants guide](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/webinar/registrants)).

**Operation:** [`rcwRegCreateRegistrant`](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/Registrants/rcwRegCreateRegistrant)  
**Endpoint:** `POST https://platform.ringcentral.com/webinar/registration/v1/sessions/{sessionId}/registrants`

Required body fields: **`firstName`**, **`lastName`**, **`email`**

```javascript
platform.post('/webinar/registration/v1/sessions/' + sessionId + '/registrants', {
  firstName: 'Luke',
  lastName: 'Skywalker',
  email: 'luke.skywalker@jedi.org',
  externalId: 'crm-lead-123',           // correlate to CRM/MAP
  source: 'website-campaign-123',       // track campaign source
  joinUri: 'https://acme.com/webinar/1234/join?id=5678',       // optional custom join page
  cancellationUri: 'https://acme.com/webinar/1234/cancel?id=5678'  // optional custom cancel page
})
```

The response includes a unique registrant **`id`** and a personalized **`joinUri`** (unless you supply your own).

### Typical custom registration flow

1. Host a sign-up page on your site (e.g. `https://acme.com/webinar/1234/join`).
2. On form submit, call **`rcwRegCreateRegistrant`** from your backend.
3. RingCentral assigns a registrant ID (e.g. `"5678"`).
4. Send the attendee their unique join link (from the API response or your custom `joinUri`).
5. Track attendance: a registrant with both **`id`** and **`participantId`** attended; no **`participantId`** means they never joined.

### Registration session preferences

Manage registration behavior via [`rcwRegUpdateSession`](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/Registration-Management/rcwRegUpdateSession):

| Preference | Purpose |
|------------|---------|
| `registrationStatus` | Turn registration on/off for the session |
| `registrantCount` | Count of registered attendees |
| `autoCloseLimit` | Close registration after N registrants |
| `suppressEmails` | Suppress RingCentral registration emails (useful when you send your own) |
| `preventMultipleDeviceJoins` | Block joining from multiple devices |

Registration must be **open** for the session; otherwise create-registrant calls return **HTTP 403**.

---

## Related: Inviting Hosts, Cohosts, and Panelists

This is separate from attendee registration. Use the invitees endpoint:

`POST /webinar/configuration/v1/webinars/{webinarId}/sessions/{sessionId}/invitees`

```javascript
platform.post(endpoint, {
  addedInvitees: [{
    firstName: 'Leia',
    lastName: 'Organa',
    email: 'leia.organa@newrepublic.gov',
    role: 'Cohost',       // e.g. Cohost, Panelist
    sendInvite: true
  }]
})
```

---

## End-to-end workflow summary

| Step | API | Key output |
|------|-----|------------|
| 1. Authenticate | JWT login | Access token |
| 2. Create webinar | `POST /webinar/configuration/v1/webinars` | `webinarId` |
| 3. Schedule session | `POST .../webinars/{webinarId}/sessions` | `sessionId` |
| 4. (Optional) Configure registration | `rcwRegUpdateSession` | Registration preferences |
| 5. Register attendees | `POST /webinar/registration/v1/sessions/{sessionId}/registrants` | Registrant `id`, `joinUri` |
| 6. (Optional) Invite facilitators | `POST .../sessions/{sessionId}/invitees` | Host/cohost/panelist invites |

---

## Additional resources

- [Creating webinars using the API](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/webinar/creation)
- [Registering attendees for a webinar](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/webinar/registrants)
- [Webinar Quick Start](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/webinar/quick-start)
- [Webinar API Reference](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/webinar)

**Note:** The Webinar API is currently in **beta**. Backwards compatibility is not guaranteed, and a Webinar license is required.

results.question: What types of events can I subscribe to using the RingCentral push notification service? Name at least 5 distinct event categories.
results.ground_truth: RingCentral subscriptions support: (1) Telephony/call events — active call sessions and state changes; (2) Message events — incoming SMS, voicemail, fax via /message-store; (3) Presence events — user presence/availability changes; (4) Extension/account events — extension updates; (5) Call queue events — queue member activity; (6) AI Conversation Expert events — ACE analysis complete; (7) Meeting/video events. All subscriptions are created via POST /restapi/v1.0/subscription with an eventFilters array. WebSocket or webhook delivery.

raw.answer:
RingCentral’s push notification service (Subscriptions API) lets you subscribe to many event types via **`eventFilters`** when you create a subscription with **`POST /restapi/v1.0/subscription`**. Delivery can be **webhook** or **WebSocket**.

Here are **7 distinct event categories** documented in this monorepo:

### 1. **Message events**
Notifications for new or updated messages in the message store, including:
- **SMS received**
- **Voicemail received**
- **Fax received**

Example filter: `/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/message-store/instant?type=SMS`

### 2. **Telephony / call events**
Real-time call lifecycle and session changes, such as:
- **Call started / call ended**
- **Missed call**
- **Telephony session state changes** (via `/telephony/sessions`)

These are preferred over polling the call log for real-time use cases.

### 3. **Presence events**
Changes to a user’s **presence/availability** (e.g., Available, Do Not Disturb).

Example filter: `/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/presence`

### 4. **AI Conversation Expert (ACE) events**
Notifications when **ACE finishes analyzing** a recorded voice call or video meeting, including transcript, summary, sentiment, etc.

### 5. **Webinar / meeting events**
Webinar-specific events such as:
- Session created or modified
- Session state changes (start/end)
- Registration setting changes
- Registrant created or modified

These use filters like `/webinar/configuration/v1/company/sessions` and related paths.

### 6. **Subscription lifecycle events**
Meta-events about your subscription itself, e.g. reminders before a webhook subscription expires:

`/restapi/v1.0/subscription/~?threshold=XXX&interval=YYY`

### 7. **Team Messaging (Glip) bot events**
For bots, events such as bot extension creation, personal chat creation, group membership, and posts/groups activity.

---

The notifications overview in `docs/notifications/index.mdx` summarizes the core categories (messages, calls, presence). For the full list of event filters, the docs point to the [RingCentral API Reference](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/Account-Presence-Event) (e.g., Account Telephony Sessions, Extension Telephony Sessions, and related event types).

mintlify.answer:
The RingCentral push notification service lets apps subscribe to platform events via **webhooks** (server-side, requires a public HTTPS endpoint) or **WebSockets** (client-side, low latency). Subscriptions are created through the Subscription API:

```http
POST /restapi/v1.0/subscription
```

For mobile/client apps, the docs recommend the **`@ringcentral/subscriptions`** package:

```bash
npm install @ringcentral/subscriptions --save
```

You specify which events you want in the `eventFilters` parameter. Each filter maps to a specific event type.

---

## Event categories you can subscribe to

Per the [Events and Notifications](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/notifications/index) documentation, RingCentral supports a broad set of events. Here are **6+ distinct categories**:

### 1. **Messaging events (SMS, voicemail, fax)**
Message Store instant events notify you when new messages arrive.

| Event | Example event filter |
|---|---|
| SMS received | `/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/message-store/instant?type=SMS` |
| Voicemail received | Message Store instant filter with `type=VoiceMail` |
| Fax received | Message Store event filter (see [Receiving Faxes](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/messaging/fax/receiving-faxes)) |

### 2. **Telephony / call events**
Real-time call lifecycle notifications:

| Event | Example event filter |
|---|---|
| Call started / call ended | `/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/telephony/sessions` |
| Account-level telephony | [Account Telephony Sessions Event](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/Account-Telephony-Sessions-Event) |
| Extension-level telephony | [Extension Telephony Sessions Event](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/Extension-Telephony-Sessions-Event) |
| Missed call | Supported as a distinct event type |

### 3. **Presence / availability events**
Notifications when a user’s availability changes (on a call, in a meeting, DND, etc.):

- **Presence/availability changed**
- API reference: [Account Presence Event](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/Account-Presence-Event)

Related subscription error codes include `SUB-402` (presence disabled) and `SUB-403` (user disallowed presence monitoring).

### 4. **Team Messaging events**
For bots and integrations that react to chat activity:

- **Posts events** (new messages in chats)
- **Groups/teams events** (team creation, bot added to groups, etc.)

Bot walkthrough docs describe subscribing to **“posts and groups events”** for Team Messaging bots.

### 5. **AI Conversation Expert (ACE) events**
Notifications when AI conversational analysis completes on recorded voice calls or video meetings:

- Requires an active ACE license
- Event filters documented in [ACE event filter](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/ai/ace) docs
- Payload includes `sourceRecordId`, `sourceSessionId`, and insights data

### 6. **Webinar events**
Webinar-specific webhook filters (see [Webinar events](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/webinar/events)):

| Event filter | Description |
|---|---|
| `/webinar/configuration/v1/company/sessions` | Session created or modified |
| `/webinar/runtime/v1/company/sessions/state` | Webinar state changes (start/end) |
| `/webinar/registration/v1/company/sessions/state` | Registration settings modified |
| `/webinar/registration/v1/company/sessions/registrants` | Registrant created or modified |

Optional filter parameters: `webinarId`, `hostUserId`.

### 7. **Subscription lifecycle events** (bonus)
Meta-events about your subscriptions themselves:

```
/restapi/v1.0/subscription/~?threshold=XXX&interval=YYY
```

Used to get renewal reminders before a webhook subscription expires.

---

## Delivery mechanisms

| Mechanism | Package | Public server required |
|---|---|---|
| **WebSocket** | `@ringcentral/subscriptions` | No |
| **Webhook** | — (Subscription API) | Yes (HTTPS) |

Both use the same Subscription API (`POST /restapi/v1.0/subscription`) and the same `eventFilters` concept. Webhook setup requires echoing the **`Validation-Token`** header during subscription validation.

---

## Summary

At minimum, the docs explicitly list these **6 core event types**:

1. SMS received  
2. Voicemail received  
3. Fax received  
4. Call started / call ended  
5. Missed call  
6. Presence/availability changed  

Beyond those, you can also subscribe to **Team Messaging**, **ACE/AI**, **Webinar**, and **subscription lifecycle** events. The [API Reference](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/Account-Presence-Event) is the definitive catalog of all available event filters.

results.question: Show me how to send an SMS using the RingCentral REST API in both JavaScript/Node.js and Ruby. What is the endpoint and key request fields?
results.ground_truth: Endpoint: POST /restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms. Required fields: from.phoneNumber, to[].phoneNumber, text. JavaScript: platform.post('/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms', {from: {phoneNumber: '...'}, to: [{phoneNumber: '...'}], text: 'Hello'}) using @ringcentral/sdk. Ruby: platform.post('/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms', payload: {from: {phoneNumber: '...'}, to: [{phoneNumber: '...'}], text: 'Hello'}) using ringcentral_sdk gem.

raw.answer:
To send an SMS with the RingCentral REST API, POST to the SMS endpoint with `from`, `to`, and `text` in the JSON body.

## Endpoint

```
POST /restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms
```

Full URL (production):

```
https://platform.ringcentral.com/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms
```

The `~` placeholders resolve to the account and extension tied to your access token. You need a valid OAuth/JWT access token and the **SMS** permission on your app.

## Key request fields

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `from.phoneNumber` | string | Yes | Sender number in E.164 format (e.g. `+15551234567`). Must be an SMS-capable number assigned to the authenticated extension (`SmsSender` feature). |
| `to[].phoneNumber` | string[] | Yes | One or more recipient numbers in E.164 format. |
| `text` | string | Yes | Message body (max 1000 UTF-16 symbols). |

Example JSON body:

```json
{
  "from": { "phoneNumber": "+15551234567" },
  "to": [{ "phoneNumber": "+15559876543" }],
  "text": "Hello!"
}
```

---

## JavaScript / Node.js (`@ringcentral/sdk`)

```bash
npm install @ringcentral/sdk dotenv
```

```javascript
require('dotenv').config()
const { SDK } = require('@ringcentral/sdk')

const rcsdk = new SDK({
    server: 'https://platform.ringcentral.com',
    clientId: process.env.RC_APP_CLIENT_ID,
    clientSecret: process.env.RC_APP_CLIENT_SECRET
})

const platform = rcsdk.platform()
await platform.login({ jwt: process.env.RC_USER_JWT })

const response = await platform.post('/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms', {
    from: { phoneNumber: '+15551234567' },
    to: [{ phoneNumber: '+15559876543' }],
    text: 'Hello!'
})

const message = await response.json()
console.log(message.id)
```

From the official JS SDK README:

```731:739:sdks/ringcentral-js/sdk/README.md
rcsdk
    .post('/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms', {
        from: {phoneNumber: '+12223334444'}, // Your sms-enabled phone number
        to: [
            {phoneNumber: '+15556667777'}, // Second party's phone number
        ],
        text: 'Message content',
    })
```

---

## Ruby (`ringcentral-sdk` gem)

```bash
gem install ringcentral-sdk
```

```ruby
require 'ringcentral'

rc = RingCentral.new('CLIENT_ID', 'CLIENT_SECRET', 'https://platform.ringcentral.com')
rc.authorize(jwt: 'YOUR_JWT_TOKEN')

r = rc.post('/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms', payload: {
    from: { phoneNumber: '+15551234567' },
    to: [{ phoneNumber: '+15559876543' }],
    text: 'Hello world'
})
```

From the official Ruby SDK README:

```133:138:sdks/ringcentral-ruby/README.md
r = rc.post('/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms', payload: {
    to: [{phoneNumber: ENV['RINGCENTRAL_RECEIVER']}],
    from: {phoneNumber: ENV['RINGCENTRAL_SENDER']},
    text: 'Hello world'
})
```

For POST requests in Ruby, pass the JSON body via the `payload:` keyword argument.

---

## Notes

- SMS can only be sent from numbers owned by (or explicitly assigned to) the authenticated user extension — see `docs/messaging/sms/sending-sms.mdx`.
- Production accounts typically require [TCR registration](https://support.ringcentral.com/article-v2/Setting-up-TCR-registration-assigning-numbers-to-SMS-campaigns.html) before sending SMS.
- The same endpoint also supports MMS when you send `multipart/form-data` with attachments (Ruby: add a `files:` argument; JS: use `FormData`).

mintlify.answer:
Here is how to send an SMS with the RingCentral REST API, based on the [SMS Quick Start](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/messaging/quick-start) and [Sending SMS](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/messaging/sms/sending-sms) documentation.

## Endpoint

| Item | Value |
|------|-------|
| **Method** | `POST` |
| **Path** | `/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms` |
| **Production base URL** | `https://platform.ringcentral.com` |
| **Sandbox base URL** | `https://platform.devtest.ringcentral.com` |

The `~` placeholders resolve to the authenticated account and extension.

**Full production URL example:**
```
POST https://platform.ringcentral.com/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms
```

## Key request fields

Send a JSON body with these fields:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `from.phoneNumber` | string | Yes | Sender number owned by the authenticated extension and enabled for SMS (`features` includes `"SmsSender"`) |
| `to` | array | Yes | Recipients; each item is `{ "phoneNumber": "+1XXXXXXXXXX" }` (up to 10 for group SMS) |
| `text` | string | Yes | Message body |

**Example request body:**
```json
{
  "from": { "phoneNumber": "+16505550100" },
  "to": [
    { "phoneNumber": "+16505550200" }
  ],
  "text": "Hello World!"
}
```

**Required HTTP headers:**
- `Content-Type: application/json`
- `Accept: application/json`
- `Authorization: Bearer {access_token}` (recommended auth method per the [tokens guide](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/authentication/tokens))

## Prerequisites

Before calling the SMS endpoint:

1. **Authenticate** a user extension (JWT is used in the quick start).
2. **App permissions:** `SMS` and `ReadAccounts`.
3. **Discover a valid sender number** via:
   ```
   GET /restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/phone-number
   ```
   Pick a number whose `features` array includes `"SmsSender"`.
4. **TCR registration** is required for production SMS (and even developer/test accounts).

---

## JavaScript / Node.js

**Install:**
```bash
npm install @ringcentral/sdk dotenv --save
```

**Package:** `@ringcentral/sdk`

**Example (`sms.js`):**
```javascript
const RC = require('@ringcentral/sdk').SDK
require('dotenv').config()

const RECIPIENT = process.env.SMS_RECIPIENT

const rcsdk = new RC({
  server: process.env.RC_SERVER_URL || 'https://platform.ringcentral.com',
  clientId: process.env.RC_APP_CLIENT_ID,
  clientSecret: process.env.RC_APP_CLIENT_SECRET
})
const platform = rcsdk.platform()

platform.login({ jwt: process.env.RC_USER_JWT })

platform.on(platform.events.loginSuccess, () => {
  read_extension_phone_number_detect_sms_feature()
})

async function read_extension_phone_number_detect_sms_feature() {
  const resp = await platform.get('/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/phone-number')
  const jsonObj = await resp.json()

  for (const record of jsonObj.records) {
    if (record.features.includes('SmsSender')) {
      return send_sms(record.phoneNumber)
    }
  }
  console.log("No SMS-capable phone number found")
}

async function send_sms(fromNumber) {
  const bodyParams = {
    from: { phoneNumber: fromNumber },
    to: [{ phoneNumber: RECIPIENT }],
    text: 'Hello World!'
  }

  const resp = await platform.post(
    '/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms',
    bodyParams
  )
  const jsonObj = await resp.json()
  console.log('SMS sent. Message id:', jsonObj.id)
}

platform.on(platform.events.loginError, (e) => {
  console.log('Unable to authenticate:', e.message)
  process.exit(1)
})
```

**Run:**
```bash
node sms.js
```

**Environment variables** (from the [env template](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ringcentral/ringcentral-api-docs/main/code-samples/env-template)):
- `RC_APP_CLIENT_ID`
- `RC_APP_CLIENT_SECRET`
- `RC_USER_JWT`
- `RC_SERVER_URL` (e.g. `https://platform.ringcentral.com`)
- `SMS_RECIPIENT`

---

## Ruby

**Install:**
```bash
gem install ringcentral_sdk dotenv
```

Per the [SDKs page](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/sdks), the package name is `ringcentral_sdk`. The SMS quick start also references `ringcentral-sdk`; use `require 'ringcentral'` in code.

**Example (`sms.rb`):**
```ruby
require 'ringcentral'
require 'dotenv'

Dotenv.load

RECIPIENT = ENV['SMS_RECIPIENT']

$platform = RingCentral.new(
  ENV['RC_APP_CLIENT_ID'],
  ENV['RC_APP_CLIENT_SECRET'],
  ENV['RC_SERVER_URL'] || 'https://platform.ringcentral.com'
)

def read_extension_phone_number_detect_sms_feature
  resp = $platform.get('/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/phone-number')

  resp.body['records'].each do |record|
    if record['features'].include?('SmsSender')
      return send_sms(record['phoneNumber'])
    end
  end

  puts 'No SMS-capable phone number found'
end

def send_sms(fromNumber)
  bodyParams = {
    from: { phoneNumber: fromNumber },
    to: [{ phoneNumber: RECIPIENT }],
    text: 'Hello World!'
  }

  resp = $platform.post(
    '/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms',
    payload: bodyParams
  )

  puts "SMS sent. Message id: #{resp.body['id']}"
end

def login
  $platform.authorize(jwt: ENV['RC_USER_JWT'])
  read_extension_phone_number_detect_sms_feature
rescue StandardError => e
  puts "Unable to authenticate: #{e}"
end

login
```

**Run:**
```bash
ruby sms.rb
```

---

## Typical flow (both languages)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant App
    participant RC as RingCentral API

    App->>RC: Authenticate (JWT)
    App->>RC: GET /account/~/extension/~/phone-number
    RC-->>App: Numbers with features (incl. SmsSender)
    App->>RC: POST /account/~/extension/~/sms
    Note over App,RC: from, to[], text
    RC-->>App: Message object (id, messageStatus)
```

After sending, you can poll delivery status with:
```
GET /restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/message-store/{messageId}
```

---

## Common errors

| HTTP | Error Code | Meaning |
|------|------------|---------|
| 403 | `MSG-242` | Sending SMS is not available from the number specified |
| 403 | `MSG-304` | Phone number doesn't belong to extension |
| 400 | `MSG-245` | Cannot find the phone number which belongs to user |
| 429 | `MSG-305` | Request rate exceeded |

See the full list in the [Messaging API error codes](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/basics/errors#messaging-api-error-codes) section.

**Docs references:**
- [SMS Quick Start](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/messaging/quick-start)
- [Sending SMS](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/messaging/sms/sending-sms)
- [SDK install commands](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/sdks)

results.question: What CRM platforms does RingCentral have native phone integration support for, and what does the rc-unified-crm-extension framework provide?
results.ground_truth: RingCentral has native phone integrations for: HubSpot (hubspot-embeddable-ringcentral-phone), Pipedrive (pipedrive-embeddable-ringcentral-phone), and Redtail (redtail-embeddable-ringcentral-phone). The rc-unified-crm-extension (App Connect) is a developer SDK/framework for building custom CRM adapters — it provides standardized call logging, contact lookup, and click-to-dial capabilities. Developers use it to connect any CRM to the RingCentral embeddable phone widget.

raw.answer:
## Native phone integrations

RingCentral ships **three dedicated embeddable-phone integrations** under `crm/` — Chrome extensions that embed the RingCentral phone widget directly into CRM web UIs:

| CRM | Repository |
|-----|------------|
| **HubSpot** | `hubspot-embeddable-ringcentral-phone` |
| **Pipedrive** | `pipedrive-embeddable-ringcentral-phone` |
| **Redtail** | `redtail-embeddable-ringcentral-phone` |

These are built with the [RingCentral Embeddable extension factory](https://github.com/ringcentral/ringcentral-embeddable-extension-factory) and add the embeddable phone to CRM contact pages. Typical features include click-to-call, inbound caller pop-ups, and auto/manual sync of call logs, voicemails, and SMS back to the CRM.

There is also a separate `ringcentral-integration-for-hubspot` repo for a fuller HubSpot integration, but the three embeddable repos above are the native per-CRM phone widgets listed in the monorepo.

---

## `rc-unified-crm-extension` (App Connect)

**App Connect** is a broader developer framework — not limited to those three CRMs — for connecting CRMs to RingCentral telephony. The docs describe it as focused on **logging phone calls** and building CRM connectors.

It provides:

- **Standardized CRM adapter interfaces** — contact lookup, call logging, click-to-dial, message logging, OAuth/API-key auth, and related connector lifecycle hooks
- **A connector SDK** (`@app-connect/core`) with a CRM connector registry, Express-based server routes, contact management, and call/message logging APIs
- **A CLI** (`@app-connect/cli init`) to scaffold custom connector servers from a template
- **A Chrome/Edge extension** that end users install to use connected CRMs with the embedded phone
- **Built-in connectors** for additional platforms (e.g. Pipedrive, Insightly, Clio, Redtail, Bullhorn, NetSuite, Google Sheets, GoHighLevel, Freshdesk) via `manifest.json`

Developers use App Connect to **build custom CRM adapters** and connect **any CRM** (with sufficient API surface) to the RingCentral embeddable phone widget — extending beyond the three native embeddable extensions.

mintlify.answer:
## Native CRM phone integrations

RingCentral’s **App Connect** product (built on the **`rc-unified-crm-extension`** project, formerly called the “Unified CRM extension”) provides **native phone integration** via a **single browser extension** for **Google Chrome** and **Microsoft Edge**. It embeds a full RingEX communications stack (phone, SMS, fax, voicemail, etc.) directly into CRM web apps.

The RingCentral Developer docs ([SDKs → Specialized SDKs](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/sdks#specialized-sdks)) point to the full integration catalog at [App Connect for RingEX](https://ringcentral.github.io/rc-unified-crm-extension/). That marketplace currently lists **16+ native integrations**:

| Platform | Category |
|---|---|
| **AgencyZoom** | Insurance agency management |
| **Bullhorn** | Recruiting & staffing ATS |
| **Clio** | Legal practice management |
| **Freshdesk** | Customer support / CTI |
| **GoHighLevel** | Marketing & sales |
| **Google Sheets** | Universal spreadsheet fallback |
| **Insightly** | CRM |
| **monday.com** | Work OS / project management |
| **NetSuite** | ERP / CRM |
| **Odoo** | ERP / CRM |
| **Pipedrive** | Sales CRM |
| **Redtail CRM** | Financial services |
| **ServiceNow** | ITSM / enterprise |
| **ServiceTitan** | Field service management |
| **Smokeball** | Legal practice management |
| **Zoho CRM** | CRM |

**App Connect 2.0 Beta** adds or expands support for **ServiceTitan**, **Smokeball**, and **Zoho CRM**, among others.

The framework is also **extensible**: developers can build connectors for proprietary or niche CRMs using the App Connect developer framework (JSON proxy config or Node.js connector server).

> **Note on Salesforce / HubSpot:** The Mintlify MCP/App Connect docs ([App Connect server](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/mcp/servers/app-connect)) mention **Salesforce**, **HubSpot**, and **Zoho** as supported CRM platforms for the **App Connect MCP server** (`getPublicConnectors`). Those are part of the broader App Connect connector ecosystem, but they are **not** listed among the native browser-extension marketplace integrations above. For the authoritative live connector list, use the `getPublicConnectors` MCP tool against `https://unified-crm-extension.labs.ringcentral.com/mcp`.

---

## What `rc-unified-crm-extension` provides

The Mintlify docs describe it under **App Connect** as:

> *“A developer framework designed around helping connect to CRMs for the primary purpose of logging phone calls.”*  
> — [SDKs → Specialized SDKs](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/sdks#specialized-sdks)

In practice, the **`rc-unified-crm-extension`** repo is the open-source **App Connect** platform. It provides two things:

### 1. End-user browser extension
A Chrome/Edge extension that:
- Embeds RingCentral calling directly inside CRM pages
- Supports **click-to-dial**, **screen-pop** (caller lookup on inbound calls), **automatic and manual call logging**, **SMS logging**, voicemail, fax, and more
- Keeps working even when the CRM tab is closed (“always on” phone)
- Runs an MCP server at **`https://unified-crm-extension.labs.ringcentral.com/mcp`** for AI-assisted CRM workflows (contact lookup, call logging, etc.)

### 2. Developer framework for building CRM connectors
Built on top of **RingCentral Embeddable**, the framework lets developers connect RingCentral to any CRM via:

| Connector mode | Use case |
|---|---|
| **Code connector** | Node.js server with custom CRM logic via `connectorRegistry.registerConnector()` |
| **Proxy connector** | Low-code JSON REST mapping in the [Developer Console](https://appconnect.labs.ringcentral.com/console/) |
| **Interface-only connector** | Register individual interface functions without replacing the full connector |

Each connector has:
- A **manifest** (auth, CRM URLs, log form fields, settings)
- A **server implementation** exporting standard interfaces like `getUserInfo`, `findContact`, `createContact`, `createCallLog`, `updateCallLog`, `createMessageLog`, etc.

The shared runtime in **`@app-connect/core`** handles HTTP routes, OAuth/token refresh, server-side logging, contact caching, plugins, and MCP/appointment surfaces.

**Key packages and install commands:**

| Package / tool | Purpose | Install |
|---|---|---|
| **`@app-connect/cli`** | Scaffold a new connector | `npx @app-connect/cli init my-crm-connector` |
| **`@app-connect/core`** | Shared connector runtime | Included in generated project; `npm install` |
| **`rc-unified-crm-extension-server`** | Main server package (repo root) | Internal monorepo package |

**Scaffold a connector:**
```bash
npx @app-connect/cli init my-crm-connector
cd my-crm-connector
npm install
cp .env.test .env
npm run dev
```

Then register the connector in the Developer Console at **`https://appconnect.labs.ringcentral.com/console/`** and expose your local server via an HTTPS tunnel.

---

**Summary:** RingCentral natively integrates its phone/communications stack into **16+ CRM platforms** through the App Connect browser extension. The **`rc-unified-crm-extension`** framework is the open-source foundation behind that product — it delivers the end-user extension plus a developer platform (`@app-connect/cli`, `@app-connect/core`) for building, deploying, and extending CRM connectors with standardized call logging, contact matching, and communication workflows.

results.question: How long does a RingCentral OAuth access token last, and how do I get a new one without forcing the user to log in again? What happens when the refresh token also expires?
results.ground_truth: Access tokens last approximately 3600 seconds (1 hour). Use the refresh_token grant type (grant_type=refresh_token) to get a new access token silently before or after expiry — no user interaction needed. Refresh tokens for Auth Code flow last 7 days. If the refresh token expires, the user must re-authenticate from scratch (full OAuth flow). For JWT (server-to-server) flows there is no refresh token — simply request a new access token using the JWT grant at any time. Always cache and reuse tokens; calling the token endpoint too frequently triggers the Auth rate limit (5 requests/user/minute).

raw.answer:
## Access token lifetime

RingCentral OAuth **access tokens last about 1 hour** (roughly 3600 seconds). The token response includes `expires_in`, which you should treat as the source of truth for when to refresh.

Some docs mention ~7200 seconds; the dedicated refresh-token page lists **1 hour** for access tokens.

---

## Getting a new access token without re-login

For **Authorization Code** (or **Auth Code + PKCE**) apps, use the **refresh token flow** — no user interaction is required.

1. **Enable refresh tokens** in your app’s Developer Console settings (“Issue refresh tokens”). They are not issued by default.

2. **Store both tokens** after the initial authorization, along with issue time and the `expires_in` / `refresh_token_expires_in` values from the response.

3. **Refresh proactively** before the access token expires (don’t wait for HTTP 401):

```http
POST /restapi/oauth/token HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Authorization: Basic <base64(clientId:clientSecret)>

grant_type=refresh_token&refresh_token=<REFRESH_TOKEN>
```

4. **Update stored tokens** with the new access token (and usually a new refresh token) from the response.

Important behaviors:

- Refresh tokens are **single-use**; each refresh typically returns a **new refresh token** — always persist the latest pair.
- Refreshing invalidates the previous access token immediately.
- For long-running/background apps, run a **scheduled job at least once per day** to refresh tokens before they lapse.
- Cache and reuse access tokens; the Auth endpoint is rate-limited to **5 requests/user/minute**.

RingCentral SDKs (JS, Python, etc.) can handle refresh automatically if you persist token state.

---

## When the refresh token also expires

For Auth Code flow, **refresh tokens last 7 days**. If you don’t refresh within that window, **both tokens expire** and there is no silent recovery — the user must **re-authenticate through the full OAuth flow** (redirect to login/consent, exchange a new authorization code).

The refresh request will fail (typically HTTP 400), and you should prompt the user to log in again.

Tokens can also be invalidated before expiry if:

- The user revokes app access
- The user’s password changes
- An admin enforces an absolute session timeout
- The app hits the limit of parallel active sessions (~5 per user)

---

## Alternative: JWT (server-to-server)

For apps with **no user UI** acting on a **single service account**, the **JWT auth flow** does not use refresh tokens. The JWT credential itself doesn’t expire (unless you configure it to), and you simply request a new access token by POSTing the JWT to `/restapi/oauth/token` with `grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer` — again, no user login required.

JWT is **not** suited for multi-user apps where each end user needs their own tokens; use Auth Code + PKCE for that.

mintlify.answer:
## Token lifetimes

Per the [Refresh tokens](https://ringcentral.mintlify.app/authentication/refresh-tokens) documentation, OAuth tokens typically expire on this schedule:

| Token | Time to live |
|-------|--------------|
| **Access token** | **1 hour** |
| **Refresh token** | **7 days** |

The token response includes `expires_in` (access token lifetime in seconds) and `refresh_token_expires_in` (refresh token lifetime in seconds). Store both along with the issue timestamp and use them to refresh **before** tokens expire — the docs recommend against waiting for `HTTP 401` errors to trigger a refresh.

> **Note:** Some other pages mention `~7200` seconds (~2 hours) for access tokens. The dedicated refresh-token page lists **1 hour / 7 days** as the standard schedule; treat the returned `expires_in` and `refresh_token_expires_in` values as authoritative for your session.

---

## Getting a new access token without re-login

Use the **refresh token flow**. This requires that your app has **"Issue refresh tokens"** enabled in the Developer Console (disabled by default).

### API request

**Endpoint:** `POST https://platform.ringcentral.com/restapi/oauth/token`

**Headers:**

| Header | Value |
|--------|-------|
| `Content-Type` | `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` |
| `Authorization` | `Basic ` + base64(`Client ID:Client Secret`) — required for server-side apps; **not** sent for "Client-side web app" type |

**POST body:**

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| `grant_type` | `refresh_token` |
| `refresh_token` | The refresh token from the prior token response |
| `client_id` | Required only for "Client-side web app" type |

Example:

```http
POST /restapi/oauth/token HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Authorization: Basic <base64-encoded ClientID:ClientSecret>

refresh_token=BCMDFUMDRKV1MwMXx5d5dwzLFL4ec6U1A0XMsUv935527jghj48
  &grant_type=refresh_token
```

On success you get `HTTP 200` with a new `access_token`, a new `refresh_token`, and updated `expires_in` / `refresh_token_expires_in`. **Always persist the new refresh token** — refresh tokens are **single-use**.

### Recommended pattern for long-lived sessions

1. Store the access token, refresh token, and both expiration timestamps.
2. Before each API call (or on a schedule), check whether the access token is close to expiring and refresh proactively.
3. Run a **background job at least once per day** that refreshes all stored tokens — if done reliably, users should not need to log in again.
4. For multithreaded apps, use a dedicated token-refresh service to avoid race conditions.

### Using the SDK

Official SDKs include refresh support. Example packages:

| Language | Package | Install |
|----------|---------|---------|
| JavaScript/Node | `@ringcentral/sdk` | `npm install @ringcentral/sdk` |
| Python | `ringcentral` | `pip3 install ringcentral` |
| Ruby | `ringcentral_sdk` | `gem install ringcentral_sdk` |

In SDK code you can call `platform.refresh()` periodically (e.g., daily or every 6 days) to keep the refresh token from expiring.

---

## What happens when the refresh token also expires?

If the refresh token reaches its **7-day** lifetime without being used to obtain a new access token:

1. **The refresh request fails** — you can no longer call `/restapi/oauth/token` with `grant_type=refresh_token`.
2. **Both tokens are dead** — the docs state that if no refresh happens for more than a week, both the access and refresh tokens expire.
3. **The user must re-authenticate** — send them through the full OAuth authorization flow again (Auth Code + PKCE is recommended for per-user apps), starting at:
   `https://platform.ringcentral.com/restapi/oauth/authorize?response_type=code&redirect_uri=<my_uri>&client_id=<client_id>`
4. **API calls with an expired access token** return **`HTTP 401 Unauthorized`**, e.g. error code **`OAU-128`** ("Access token expired").

Other events that invalidate tokens (not just TTL) and also force re-authentication:

- User revokes the app session
- User changes their password
- Admin enforces "Absolute Session Timeout"
- App hits the parallel session limit (usually **5** active sessions per user)

---

## Practical takeaway

- Access tokens last **~1 hour**; refresh tokens last **~7 days**.
- To avoid re-login: enable refresh tokens, call `POST /restapi/oauth/token` with `grant_type=refresh_token`, save the new tokens, and refresh proactively (ideally daily).
- If the refresh token expires unused for 7 days, there is no silent recovery — the user must complete the OAuth login/consent flow again.

results.question: Which RingCentral SDKs have a dedicated WebSocket or real-time communication library beyond the standard REST SDK, and what is each called?
results.ground_truth: Beyond the standard REST SDKs, RingCentral provides: (1) @ringcentral/subscriptions — Node.js npm package for WebSocket push subscriptions (install: npm install @ringcentral/subscriptions); (2) ringcentral-websocket-java — Java WebSocket client library; (3) ringcentral-softphone-ts / ringcentral-softphone-js / ringcentral-softphone-go — SIP/WebRTC softphone SDKs for audio streaming in TypeScript, JavaScript, and Go respectively; (4) ringcentral-web-phone — full WebRTC-based browser phone SDK. The standard REST SDKs (Python, PHP, Ruby, .NET, Java) handle REST calls only; WebSocket/real-time needs a separate library.

raw.answer:
RingCentral splits **REST** and **real-time** into separate libraries in most languages. The standard REST SDKs (Python, PHP, Ruby, Go, Swift, and the core `@ringcentral/sdk`, `RingCentral.Net`, and `ringcentral-java` packages) handle HTTP API calls only. WebSocket event subscriptions and voice/WebRTC each have their own add-on libraries.

## WebSocket subscription libraries (platform event push)

These subscribe to RingCentral events (calls, messages, presence, etc.) over WebSocket:

| Language / platform | Library name | Package / install |
|---|---|---|
| **JavaScript / Node.js** | `@ringcentral/subscriptions` | `npm install @ringcentral/subscriptions` |
| **Java** | `ringcentral-websocket-java` | Maven: `com.ringcentral:ringcentral-websocket` |
| **.NET / C#** | `RingCentral.Net.WebSocket` | NuGet: `dotnet add package RingCentral.Net.WebSocket` |

For JavaScript, `@ringcentral/subscriptions` is a thin wrapper over the **WebSocket Extension** (`@rc-ex/ws`) in the `ringcentral-extensible` repo. The Java SDK’s README explicitly points to `ringcentral-websocket-java` as the separate WebSocket library rather than bundling it in the core SDK.

**Note:** Python, PHP, and Ruby include WebSocket subscription support inside their main SDK packages (`ringcentral`, `ringcentral/ringcentral-php`, `ringcentral-sdk`), not as separate libraries. Go and Swift have no WebSocket subscription library in this monorepo.

---

## Real-time voice / SIP / WebRTC libraries

These handle live audio, SIP signaling, and WebRTC — beyond REST:

| Language | Repo | Published name |
|---|---|---|
| **TypeScript** | `ringcentral-softphone-ts` | **`ringcentral-softphone`** (npm) — also called the **Cloud Phone SDK** |
| **JavaScript** | `ringcentral-softphone-js` | Deprecated; use `ringcentral-softphone-ts` instead |
| **Go** | `ringcentral-softphone-go` | **RingCentral Softphone SDK for Go** |
| **JavaScript (browser)** | `ringcentral-web-phone` (under `embeddable/`) | **`ringcentral-web-phone`** — WebRTC + SIP over WebSockets for browser phone clients |

---

## Summary

**Dedicated WebSocket / real-time libraries beyond the standard REST SDK:**

1. **`@ringcentral/subscriptions`** — WebSocket event subscriptions (Node.js)
2. **`ringcentral-websocket-java`** — WebSocket event subscriptions (Java)
3. **`RingCentral.Net.WebSocket`** — WebSocket event subscriptions (.NET)
4. **`ringcentral-softphone`** (`ringcentral-softphone-ts`) — server-side SIP/WebRTC audio
5. **`ringcentral-softphone-go`** — Go SIP softphone
6. **`ringcentral-web-phone`** — browser WebRTC phone SDK

Related but not standalone WebSocket libraries: **`ringcentral-call-js`** (`ringcentral-call`) combines `ringcentral-web-phone` and `ringcentral-call-control-js` for a full call-management experience, and **`ringcentral-call-control-js`** uses REST + webhooks/subscriptions rather than providing its own WebSocket layer.

mintlify.answer:
Based on the RingCentral developer documentation, only **two** of the seven official REST SDK languages ship a **separate, dedicated WebSocket library**. JavaScript also has **specialized real-time (WebRTC) libraries** that sit outside the REST SDK.

---

## Dedicated WebSocket libraries (event notifications)

These are add-on packages for subscribing to RingCentral events over WebSockets (SMS, presence, call events, etc.) via `POST /restapi/v1.0/subscription`, without needing a public webhook server.

| Language | REST SDK | Dedicated WebSocket library | Install command |
|---|---|---|---|
| **JavaScript/Node.js** | `@ringcentral/sdk` | **`@ringcentral/subscriptions`** | `npm install @ringcentral/subscriptions --save` |
| **.NET/C#** | `RingCentral.Net` | **`RingCentral.Net.WebSocket`** | Add NuGet package `RingCentral.Net.WebSocket` (used alongside `RingCentral.Net`) |

The docs describe `@ringcentral/subscriptions` as handling **WebSocket connection management, reconnection, and event subscription** — ideal for mobile and client-side apps. The C# WebSocket quick start explicitly adds both `RingCentral.Net` and `RingCentral.Net.WebSocket` as separate NuGet packages.

---

## Other REST SDKs: WebSocket built in, not separate

The WebSocket quick start covers **Java, Python, Ruby, and PHP**, but each uses only the **main REST SDK** — no separate WebSocket package is documented:

| Language | Package | Install command |
|---|---|---|
| Java | `com.ringcentral:ringcentral` | Maven dependency |
| Python | `ringcentral` | `pip3 install ringcentral` |
| Ruby | `ringcentral_sdk` | `gem install ringcentral-sdk` |
| PHP | `ringcentral/ringcentral-php` | `composer require ringcentral/ringcentral-php` |

WebSocket support in these SDKs is integrated into the standard REST library. **Go** (`github.com/ringcentral/ringcentral-go`) has no WebSocket quick start in the docs.

---

## Dedicated real-time communication libraries (WebRTC/voice)

Beyond WebSocket event notifications, RingCentral lists **Specialized SDKs** for real-time voice — all JavaScript-based and separate from `@ringcentral/sdk`:

| Library name | Repo / package identifier | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **Web Phone SDK** | `ringcentral-web-phone` | Browser WebRTC voice — SIP signaling, make/receive calls, call events |
| **Cloud Phone SDK** | `ringcentral-softphone-ts` | Real-time audio stream for a single call (recording or streaming audio into a call) |
| **RingCentral Call JS Library** | `ringcentral-call-js` | Call control across devices; built on the Web Phone SDK + Call Control REST API |

The SDKs page also lists `ringcentral-web-phone` under REST APIs as a **"JavaScript WebRTC library"**, distinct from `ringcentral-js` (the REST SDK).

---

## Summary

**Separate WebSocket libraries beyond the REST SDK:** only **Node.js** (`@ringcentral/subscriptions`) and **.NET** (`RingCentral.Net.WebSocket`).

**Separate real-time communication libraries beyond the REST SDK:** **Web Phone SDK** (`ringcentral-web-phone`), **Cloud Phone SDK** (`ringcentral-softphone-ts`), and **RingCentral Call JS Library** (`ringcentral-call-js`) — all JavaScript/WebRTC focused.

Java, Python, Ruby, PHP, and Go do **not** have a documented dedicated WebSocket or real-time add-on package; WebSocket subscriptions in those languages use the standard REST SDK.
